payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the current, non-superseded memory associated with Sam in the Goldman context. mem_old was filtered out because it is explicitly superseded, and recall should not present superseded facts as current when a newer replacement exists for the same fact scope.

entity_resolution.reason: The query explicitly scoped Sam by organization, which disambiguated the person and aligned recall to the current memory chain for Sam from Goldman.

memory_cards.statement: Sam from Goldman matched the recall query via entity and organization context, so mem_new was selected as the current card.
mem_old was filtered because it is marked superseded by a newer memory for the same underlying fact scope.

details: Entity context matched Sam from Goldman.
mem_new qualified as the active memory.
mem_old was excluded due to supersession status.
No deleted or superseded fact was presented as current.

repair_options: Verify that mem_old has an explicit superseded_by link to mem_new.
Confirm both memories resolve to the same entity scope: Sam at Goldman.
If recall should show history, expose mem_old only in an audit/debug view, not as a current result.
If filtering was unexpected, inspect whether the supersession metadata was attached too broadly.
